metadata.scale_readiness: 45 curated passages across 11 principal Upanishad names; curated explicit starter / sample-scale

documents.sanskrit: ईशा वास्यमिदं सर्वं यत्किञ्च जगत्यां जगत् । तेन त्यक्तेन भुञ्जीथा मा गृधः कस्यस्विद्धनम् ॥
documents.transliteration: īśā vāsyamidaṁ sarvaṁ yatkiñca jagatyāṁ jagat | tena tyaktena bhuñjīthā mā gṛdhaḥ kasyasviddhanam ||
documents.text: All this, whatever moves in this moving world, is enveloped by the Lord. Therefore, find your enjoyment in renunciation; do not covet what belongs to others.

documents.sanskrit: श्रोत्रस्य श्रोत्रं मनसो मनो यद्वाचो ह वाचं स उ प्राणस्य प्राणः चक्षुषश्चक्षुरतिमुच्य धीराः प्रेत्यास्माल्लोकादमृता भवन्ति ॥
documents.transliteration: śrotrasya śrotraṁ manaso mano yadvāco ha vācaṁ sa u prāṇasya prāṇaḥ cakṣuṣaścakṣuratimucya dhīrāḥ pretyāsmāllokādamṛtā bhavanti ||
documents.text: It is the ear of the ear, the mind of the mind, the speech of speech, the life of life, and the eye of the eye. The wise, freed from sense-perceptions, on leaving this world become immortal.

documents.sanskrit: केनेषितं पतति प्रेषितं मनः केन प्राणः प्रथमः प्रैति युक्तः । केनेषितां वाचमिमां वदन्ति चक्षुः श्रोत्रं क उ देवो युनक्ति ॥
documents.transliteration: keneṣitaṁ patati preṣitaṁ manaḥ kena prāṇaḥ prathamaḥ praiti yuktaḥ | keneṣitāṁ vācamimāṁ vadanti cakṣuḥ śrotraṁ ka u devo yunakti ||
documents.text: By whose will directed does the mind fly forth? By whose command does the first breath move? By whose will do people utter speech? What god directs the eye and ear?

documents.sanskrit: न तत्र चक्षुर्गच्छति न वाग्गच्छति नो मनो न विद्मो न विजानीमो यथैतदनुशिष्यात् ।
documents.transliteration: na tatra cakṣurgacchati na vāggacchati no mano na vidmo na vijānīmo yathaitadanuśiṣyāt |
documents.text: There the eye does not go, nor speech, nor the mind. We do not know, we do not understand, how anyone can teach this.

documents.sanskrit: यदि मन्यसे सुवेदेति दभ्रमेवापि नूनं त्वं वेत्थ ब्रह्मणो रूपम् । यदस्य त्वं यदस्य देवेष्वथ नु मीमांस्यमेव ते मन्ये विदितम् ॥
documents.transliteration: yadi manyase suvedeti dabhramevāpi nūnaṁ tvaṁ vettha brahmaṇo rūpam | yadasya tvaṁ yadasya deveṣvatha nu mīmāṁsyameva te manye viditam ||
documents.text: If you think 'I know Brahman well,' then surely you know very little of Its form. What you know of Brahman in yourself, what you know in the gods — that alone is worth contemplating.

documents.sanskrit: नाहं मन्ये सुवेदेति नो न वेदेति वेद च । यो नस्तद्वेद तद्वेद नो न वेदेति वेद च ॥
documents.transliteration: nāhaṁ manye suvedeti no na vedeti veda ca | yo nastadveda tadveda no na vedeti veda ca ||
documents.text: I do not think that I know Brahman well, nor do I think that I do not know It. He among us who knows what is meant by 'I neither know nor do I not know' — he knows Brahman.

documents.sanskrit: ब्रह्म ह देवेभ्यो विजिग्ये तस्य ह ब्रह्मणो विजये देवा अमहीयन्त ।
documents.transliteration: brahma ha devebhyo vijigye tasya ha brahmaṇo vijaye devā amahīyanta |
documents.text: Brahman won a victory for the gods. The gods became elated in the victory of Brahman.

documents.sanskrit: अन्यच्छ्रेयोऽन्यदुतैव प्रेयस्ते उभे नानार्थे पुरुषं सिनीतः । तयोः श्रेय आददानस्य साधु भवति हीयतेऽर्थाद्य उ प्रेयो वृणीते ॥
documents.transliteration: anyachchreyo'nyadutaiva preyaste ubhe nānārthe puruṣaṁ sinītaḥ | tayoḥ śreya ādadānasya sādhu bhavati hīyate'rthādya u preyo vṛṇīte ||
documents.text: The good (Shreya) and the pleasant (Preya) are different. Both of these bind a person. It goes well with him who clings to the good, but he who chooses the pleasant misses the true end of life.

documents.sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठत जाग्रत प्राप्य वरान्निबोधत । क्षुरस्य धारा निशिता दुरत्यया दुर्गं पथस्तत्कवयो वदन्ति ॥
documents.transliteration: uttiṣṭhata jāgrata prāpya varānnibodhata | kṣurasya dhārā niśitā duratyayā durgaṁ pathastatkavayo vadanti ||
documents.text: Arise! Awake! Approach the great teachers and learn from them. The path is as difficult to tread as a razor's edge, so say the wise.

documents.sanskrit: नायमात्मा प्रवचनेन लभ्यो न मेधया न बहुना श्रुतेन । यमेवैष वृणुते तेन लभ्यस्तस्यैष आत्मा विवृणुते तनूं स्वाम् ॥
documents.transliteration: nāyamātmā pravacanena labhyo na medhayā na bahunā śrutena | yamevaiṣa vṛṇute tena labhyastasyaiṣa ātmā vivṛṇute tanūṁ svām ||
documents.text: The Atman cannot be attained by the study of the Vedas, nor by intellect, nor by much hearing. He alone can be attained whom the Self chooses. To him the Self reveals its own form.

documents.sanskrit: ब्रह्मा देवानां प्रथमः संबभूव विश्वस्य कर्ता भुवनस्य गोप्ता ।
documents.transliteration: brahmā devānāṁ prathamaḥ saṁbabhūva viśvasya kartā bhuvanasya goptā |
documents.text: Brahma was born first among the gods, the maker of all, the protector of the world.

documents.sanskrit: द्वे विद्ये वेदितव्ये इति ह स्म यद्ब्रह्मविदो वदन्ति परा चैवापरा च ॥
documents.transliteration: dve vidye veditavye iti ha sma yadbrahmavido vadanti parā caivāparā ca ||
documents.text: There are two forms of knowledge to be known, as the knowers of Brahman are wont to say — the higher and the lower.

documents.sanskrit: यत्तदद्रेश्यमग्राह्यमगोत्रमवर्णमचक्षुःश्रोत्रं तदपाणिपादम् ।
documents.transliteration: yattadadreśyamagrāhyamgotramvarṇamacakṣuḥśrotraṁ tadapāṇipādam |
documents.text: That which cannot be seen or grasped, which has no family or caste, which has neither eyes nor ears, neither hands nor feet — the eternal, all-pervading, omnipresent, subtle — that is the Unchanging, which the wise behold as the source of all beings.

documents.sanskrit: परीक्ष्य लोकान् कर्मचितान् ब्राह्मणो निर्वेदमायात् नास्त्यकृतः कृतेन ।
documents.transliteration: parīkṣya lokān karmacitān brāhmaṇo nirvedamāyāt nāstyakṛtaḥ kṛtena |
documents.text: Having examined the worlds built by karma, a Brahmana should arrive at indifference. What is not made cannot be attained by what is made.

documents.sanskrit: तदेतत्सत्यं यथा सुदीप्तात्पावकाद्विस्फुलिङ्गाः सहस्रशः प्रभवन्ते सरूपाः ।
documents.transliteration: tdetatsatyaṁ yathā sudīptātpāvakādvisphuliṅgāḥ sahasraśaḥ prabhavante sarūpāḥ |
documents.text: This is the truth: as from a blazing fire sparks of the same nature issue forth by the thousands, so from the imperishable Brahman, diverse living beings come forth and return to Him.

documents.sanskrit: ब्रह्मैवेदं विश्वमिदं वरिष्ठम् ।
documents.transliteration: brahmaivedam viśvamidaṁ variṣṭham |
documents.text: Brahman alone is this universe — this most excellent (Brahman).

documents.sanskrit: ओमित्येतदक्षरमिदं सर्वं तस्योपव्याख्यानं भूतं भवद्भविष्यदिति सर्वमोंकार एव । यच्चान्यत् त्रिकालातीतं तदप्योंकार एव ॥
documents.transliteration: omityetadakṣaramidaṁ sarvaṁ tasyopavyākhyānaṁ bhūtaṁ bhavadbhaviṣyaditi sarvamoṁkāra eva | yaccānyat trikālātītaṁ tadapyoṁkāra eva ||
documents.text: OM — this syllable is all this. The past, the present, and the future — all that is, is but OM. And whatever transcends the three divisions of time — that also is but OM.

documents.sanskrit: नान्तःप्रज्ञं न बहिष्प्रज्ञं नोभयतःप्रज्ञं न प्रज्ञानघनं न प्रज्ञं नाप्रज्ञम् ।
documents.transliteration: nāntaḥprajñaṁ na bahiṣprajñaṁ nobhayataḥprajñaṁ na prajñānaghanaṁ na prajñaṁ nāprajñam |
documents.text: The fourth state (Turiya) is not the knower of internal objects, nor the knower of external objects, nor the knower of both, nor a mass of pure consciousness, nor the simple knower, nor the unknown.

documents.sanskrit: सर्वं ह्येतद्ब्रह्मायमात्मा ब्रह्म सोऽयमात्मा चतुष्पात् ।
documents.transliteration: sarvaṁ hyetadbrahm āyamātmā brahma so'yamātmā catuṣpāt |
documents.text: All this is, indeed, Brahman. This Atman is Brahman. This same Atman has four quarters (states of consciousness).

documents.sanskrit: जागरितस्थानो बहिष्प्रज्ञः सप्ताङ्ग एकोनविंशतिमुखः स्थूलभुग्वैश्वानरः प्रथमः पादः ॥
documents.transliteration: jāgaritasthāno bahiṣprajñaḥ saptāṅga ekonaviṁśatimukhaḥ sthūlabhugvaiśvānaraḥ prathamaḥ pādaḥ ||
documents.text: The first quarter is Vaisvanara (the waker), whose sphere of activity is the waking state, who is conscious of external objects, who has seven limbs and nineteen mouths, and who enjoys gross objects.

documents.sanskrit: स्वप्नस्थानोऽन्तःप्रज्ञः सप्ताङ्ग एकोनविंशतिमुखः प्रविविक्तभुक्तैजसो द्वितीयः पादः ॥
documents.transliteration: svapnasthāno'ntaḥprajñaḥ saptāṅga ekonaviṁśatimukhaḥ praviviktabhuktaijaso dvitīyaḥ pādaḥ ||
documents.text: The second quarter is Taijasa (the dreamer), whose sphere is the dream state, who is conscious of subtle objects, who has seven limbs and nineteen mouths, and who enjoys subtle objects.

documents.sanskrit: यत्र सुप्तो न कञ्चन कामं कामयते न कञ्चन स्वप्नं पश्यति तत्सुषुप्तम् ।
documents.transliteration: yatra supto na kañcana kāmaṁ kāmayate na kañcana svapnaṁ paśyati tatssuṣuptam |
documents.text: The state in which the sleeper desires no dreams and experiences no dreams — that is deep sleep (Sushupti).

documents.sanskrit: द्वैतस्याग्रहणं हि अद्वैतम् अग्रहणं हि तुरीयम् ।
documents.transliteration: dvaitasyāgrahaṇaṁ hi advaitam agrahaṇaṁ hi turīyam |
documents.text: Non-grasping of duality is non-duality. The non-grasping of any object whatsoever is Turiya.

documents.sanskrit: सुकेशा च भारद्वाजः शैब्यश्च सत्यकामः...
documents.transliteration: sukeśā ca bhāradvājaḥ śaibyaśca satyakāmaḥ...
documents.text: Sukesha Bharadvaja, and Saivya Satyakama... approached the revered Pippalada, thinking that he would tell them all.

documents.sanskrit: यतो वाचो निवर्तन्ते अप्राप्य मनसा सह । आनन्दं ब्रह्मणो विद्वान् न बिभेति कुतश्चन ॥
documents.transliteration: yato vāco nivartante aprāpya manasā saha | ānandaṁ brahmaṇo vidvān na bibheti kutaścana ||
documents.text: From where speech, together with the mind, turns back, unable to reach — he who knows the bliss of Brahman fears nothing at all.

documents.sanskrit: शीक्षां व्याख्यास्यामः । वर्णः स्वरः । मात्रा बलम् । साम सन्तानः । इत्युक्तः शीक्षाध्यायः ॥
documents.transliteration: śīkṣāṁ vyākhyāsyāmaḥ | varṇaḥ svaraḥ | mātrā balam | sāma santānaḥ | ityuktaḥ śīkṣādhyāyaḥ ||
documents.text: We shall expound Siksha (phonetics): phoneme, pitch, quantity, force, rhythm, and manner of junction — thus is the chapter on Siksha stated.

documents.sanskrit: ब्रह्मविदाप्नोति परम् । तदेषाऽभ्युक्ता ।
documents.transliteration: brahmāvidāpnoti param | tadeṣā'bhyuktā |
documents.text: The knower of Brahman attains the supreme. This is the verse about it.

documents.sanskrit: अन्नं ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् अन्नाद्ध्येव खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते ।
documents.transliteration: annaṁ brahmeti vyajānāt annāddhyeva khalvimāni bhūtāni jāyante |
documents.text: He realised that food is Brahman. From food, verily, are all these beings born.

documents.sanskrit: असन्नेव स भवति असद्ब्रह्मेति वेद चेत् अस्ति ब्रह्मेति चेद्वेद सन्तमेनं ततो विदुः ॥
documents.transliteration: asanneva sa bhavati asadbrahmeti veda cet asti brahmeti cedveda santamenaṁ tato viduḥ ||
documents.text: If one knows Brahman as non-existent, one becomes non-existent. If one knows Brahman as existing, then they know that he truly exists.

documents.sanskrit: अन्नं न निन्द्यात् तद्व्रतम् । प्राणो वा अन्नम् । शरीरमन्नादम् ।
documents.transliteration: annaṁ na nindyāt tadvratam | prāṇo vā annam | śarīramannādam |
documents.text: Do not despise food — that is the vow. The vital breath is food. The body eats food.

documents.sanskrit: आत्मा वा इदमेक एवाग्र आसीत् नान्यत् किंचन मिषत् । स ईक्षत लोकान्नु सृजा इति ॥
documents.transliteration: ātmā vā idameka evāgra āsīt nānyat kiṁcana miṣat | sa īkṣata lokānnu sṛjā iti ||
documents.text: In the beginning, this was the Self alone — there was nothing else that blinked. He thought, 'Let me now create the worlds.'

documents.sanskrit: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो
documents.transliteration: sa ya eṣo'ṇimaitadātmyamidaṁ sarvaṁ tatsatyaṁ sa ātmā tattvamasi śvetaketo
documents.text: That which is the finest essence — in it all that exists has its self. That is the Truth. That is the Self. And that, O Shvetaketu, is what you are. (Tat Tvam Asi)

documents.sanskrit: यो वै भूमा तत्सुखं नाल्पे सुखमस्ति भूमैव सुखं भूमा त्वेव विजिज्ञासितव्यम् ।
documents.transliteration: yo vai bhūmā tatsukhaṁ nālpe sukhamasti bhūmaiva sukhaṁ bhūmā tveva vijijñāsitavyam |
documents.text: That which is infinite (Bhuma) is happiness. There is no happiness in the finite. Only in the Infinite is there happiness.

documents.sanskrit: सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत ।
documents.transliteration: sarvaṁ khalvidaṁ brahma tajjalāniti śānta upāsīta |
documents.text: All this is Brahman. From Him all things originate, into Him all things dissolve, and in Him they breathe — meditate on that calmly.

documents.sanskrit: सदेव सोम्येदमग्र आसीदेकमेवाद्वितीयम् ।
documents.transliteration: sadeva somyedamgra āsīdekamevādvitīyam |
documents.text: In the beginning, O dear one, this was Existence (Sat) alone, one only, without a second.

documents.sanskrit: अध्यापयस्व भगव इति होपससाद सनत्कुमारं नारदः ।
documents.transliteration: adhyāpayasva bhagava iti hopasasāda sanatkumāraṁ nāradaḥ |
documents.text: Narada approached Sanatkumara and said: 'Teach me, O Revered One.'

documents.sanskrit: अथ यदिदमस्मिन् ब्रह्मपुरे दहरं पुण्डरीकं वेश्म दहरोऽस्मिन्नन्तराकाशस्तस्मिन्नेतदन्वेष्टव्यं तद्वाव विजिज्ञासितव्यम् ।
documents.transliteration: atha yadidamasmin brahma pure daharaṁ puṇḍarīkaṁ veśma daharo'sminnantarākāśastasminnetadanveṣṭavyaṁ tadvāva vijijñāsitavyam |
documents.text: Now, in this city of Brahman (the body) there is a small lotus-shaped dwelling. Within it is a small inner space. That which is within that should be sought; that should indeed be known.

documents.sanskrit: असतो मा सद्गमय तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय मृत्योर्मा अमृतं गमय ॥
documents.transliteration: asato mā sadgamaya tamaso mā jyotirgamaya mṛtyormā amṛtaṁ gamaya ||
documents.text: Lead me from the unreal to the real. Lead me from darkness to light. Lead me from death to immortality.

documents.sanskrit: अहं ब्रह्मास्मि
documents.transliteration: ahaṁ brahmāsmi
documents.text: I am Brahman (Aham Brahmasmi). This is the Mahavakya declaring the unity of the individual self with the universal Brahman.

documents.sanskrit: नेति नेति
documents.transliteration: neti neti
documents.text: Not this, not this (Neti Neti). Brahman transcends all limiting attributes — the classical Upanishadic method of approaching the Absolute through negation.

documents.sanskrit: विज्ञानमानन्दं ब्रह्म रातिर्दातुः परायणम् ।
documents.transliteration: vijñānamānandaṁ brahma rātirdātuḥ parāyaṇam |
documents.text: Brahman is pure knowledge, pure bliss — the goal of the giver of gifts, the refuge of one who knows and stands firm.

documents.sanskrit: एतस्य वा अक्षरस्य प्रशासने गार्गि सूर्यचन्द्रमसौ विधृतौ तिष्ठतः ।
documents.transliteration: etasya vā akṣarasya praśāsane gārgi sūryacandramasau vidhṛtau tiṣṭhataḥ |
documents.text: O Gargi, by the command of this Imperishable, the sun and the moon are held in their positions.

documents.sanskrit: अयं आत्मा ब्रह्म ।
documents.transliteration: ayaṁ ātmā brahma |
documents.text: This Atman is Brahman (Ayam Atma Brahma). The third of the four great sayings (Mahavakyas) — the individual soul is identical with the universal consciousness.

documents.sanskrit: मन एव मनुष्याणां कारणं बन्धमोक्षयोः ।
documents.transliteration: mana eva manuṣyāṇāṁ kāraṇaṁ bandhamokṣayoḥ |
documents.text: The mind alone is the cause of bondage and liberation for human beings.

documents.sanskrit: ब्रह्मवादिनो वदन्ति । किं कारणं ब्रह्म कुतः स्म जाता जीवाम केन क्व च सम्प्रतिष्ठाः ।
documents.transliteration: brahmavādino vadanti | kiṁ kāraṇaṁ brahma kutaḥ sma jātā jīvāma kena kva ca sampratiṣṭhāḥ |
documents.text: Those who discourse on Brahman say: What is the cause? Is it Brahman? Whence are we born? By what do we live? And on what are we established?
